---
title: "Microclimate.jl"
subtitle: "A new package and ecosystem for Microclimate modelling"
author: "Rafael Schouten — Montpellier 2026"
---

---

## Design space for a microclimate model 

We want to know the *small-scale* microclimate predictions over *large* areas, and often high temporal resolution.

::: {.incremental}
1. **Performance**: physics solves must be fast
2. **Composability**: new methods should easily drop in without editing the package
3. **Flexibility**: we want to choose resulution and algorithm quality to fit the task
:::

::: {.fragment}

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
We need levers: algorithm and data
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](figures/lever.png)
:::

:::

:::

::: {.fragment}

:::

---

# Our approach

::: {.incremental}
- **Declarative**: scripts describe *what* to solve, not *how*
- **Modular**: data sources and physics component can be swapped for alternatives
- **Univeral unit-checking**: temperatures, pressures, lengths, fluxes carry units that are checked.
- **Readable** formulations are short and look like the math in the paper
- **Speed** must be as fast or faster than existing packages
- **Landuage**: Julia
:::
---

![](figures/clbg_summary-zoomed-out.jpg){fig-align="center"}

---

## Modularity (and units) example : vapour pressure

`FluidProperties.jl` provides several `vapour_pressure` formulations. Each is a struct, pick one by passing it.

```julia
using FluidProperties, Unitful

# Three formulas, same interface
vp = vapour_pressure(GoffGratch(), 273.15u"K")
vp = vapour_pressure(Teten(), 273.15u"K") 
vp = vapour_pressure(Huang(), 273.15u"K")       

# Or we can use a lookup table
vplookup = VapourPressureLookup(GoffGratch())
vp = vapour_pressure(vplookup, 273.15u"K")
```

---

```julia
struct Teten <: VapourPressureEquation end

function vapour_pressure(::Teten, T)
    Tc = ustrip(u"°C", T)
    P_triple = 6.1071    # hPa, vapour pressure at triple point
    return P_triple * exp((17.269 * Tc) / (237.3 + Tc)) * 100u"Pa"
end
```

---

## Microclimate.jl

A Julia rewrite of the physics behind [NicheMapR](https://github.com/mrke/NicheMapR) (Kearney & Porter 2017).

::: {.incremental}
- 1-D coupled soil-energy + atmospheric-profile model.
- Heat & water transport in the soil column, Monin–Obukhov boundary layer above.
- Optional `SnowModel` with consecutive-day accumulation/melt.
- Hourly air, soil, humidity, wind at user-chosen heights and depths.
- Decoupled, composable Julia packages instead of monolithic Fortran.
:::

---

### Terminology

Model: An abstract model specification that can be run anywhere
Problem: A specification to solve in a specific spatial/temporal context

---

## Solve a single microclimate

A declarative `MicroModel` describes what physics to run: soil properties,
hydraulics, snow.

```julia
using Microclimate, Unitful

depths  = [0.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0, 15.0, 20.0, 30.0, 50.0, 100.0, 200.0]u"cm"
heights = [0.01, 2.0]u"m"

micro_model = MicroModel(;
    depths, heights,
    soil_properties_model = CampbelldeVriesSoilProperties(;
        de_vries_shape_factor = 0.1,            # 0.33 organic, 0.1 mineral
        mineral_conductivity  = 1.25u"W/m/K",
        mineral_heat_capacity = 870.0u"J/kg/K",
        recirculation_power   = 4.0,
        return_flow_threshold = 0.162,          # m³/m³
    ),
    soil_hydraulic_model = CampbellSoilHydraulics(;   # plant-side scalars
        root_resistance              = 2.5e10u"m^3/kg/s",
        stomatal_closure_potential   = -1500.0u"J/kg",
        leaf_resistance              = 2.0e6u"m^4/kg/s",
        stomatal_stability_parameter = 10.0,
        root_radius                  = 0.001u"m",
    ),
    snow_model = SnowModel(),
)
```

---

## Run one specific microclimate: `MicroProblem`

We need to define spatial/temporal inputs to pair with the model.

```julia
using Microclimate: example_site, example_monthly_weather,
    example_daily_environment, example_hourly_environment

site = example_site()

inputs = MicroInputs(;
    site,
    soil_profile = SoilProfile(;
        bulk_density = fill(2.56u"Mg/m^3", length(depths)),
        mineral_density = fill(2.56u"Mg/m^3", length(depths)),
        hydraulics = example_campbell_hydraulic_profile(depths),
    ),
    environment_minmax = example_monthly_weather(),
    environment_daily  = example_daily_environment(),
    environment_hourly = example_hourly_environment(; elevation = site.elevation),
    initial_soil_moisture = fill(0.105, length(depths)),
)

problem = MicroProblem(micro_model, inputs)
output  = solve(problem)
```

---

## Declarative data sources: MicroclimateMapper.jl

A declarative `MicroMapModel` describes what data to use with a model, and e.g. what lapse rate model.

```julia
using RasterDataSources # For SRTM, NCEP and others

map_model = MicroMapModel(micro_model;
    dem_source = SRTM,
    weather_source = NCEP{SurfaceGauss},
    surface_albedo_source = 0.15, # We can just use a constant too
    roughness_height_source = 0.004u"m", # Must have correct units!
    lapse_rate_model = EnvironmentalLapseRate(),
)
```
---

A `MicroVectorProblem` pairs it with points, the year range, the per-location
`SoilProfile`, and initial conditions.

```julia
points = [
    (3.520, 44.143),   # Camprieu (valley, ~900 m)
    (3.582, 44.115),   # South-facing slope (~1200 m)
    (3.581, 44.122),   # Mont Aigoual summit (~1567 m)
]

problem = MicroVectorProblem(map_model;
    points,
    years = 2010:2010,
    soil_profile = example_soil_profile(depths),
    init = (;
        soil_moisture = fill(0.25, length(depths)),
        snow_depth = 0.0u"cm",
    ),
)

output = solve(problem)
```

::: {.fragment}
Same NCEP cell drives all three points — terrain lapse-correction does the rest.
:::

---

## Winter snowpack — Mont Aigoual, 2010

![](figures/point_snow_depth.png){fig-align="center" width="85%"}

Daily NCEP forcing in `ConsecutiveDayMode`: snow accumulates and melts across the winter.
Same weather cell, three elevations, ≈ 50 cm difference in peak snowpack.

---

## A representative winter day

![](figures/point_temperatures.png){fig-align="center" width="85%"}

Hourly soil and air temperatures at each site for one mid-January day.
Same forcing, same physics — terrain alone splits the outcomes.

---

## Scaling up: `MicroRasterProblem`

Like MicroVectorProblme, but with a raster `template` instead of points.
This run uses hourly TerraClimate so we can watch a diurnal cycle.

```julia
area = Extent(X = (3.554, 3.608), Y = (44.095, 44.149))   # Mont Aigoual

map_model = MicroMapModel(micro_model;
    dem_source = SRTM,
    weather_source = TerraClimate{Historical},
    surface_albedo_source = 0.15,
    roughness_height_source = 0.004u"m",
)

problem = MicroRasterProblem(map_model;
    area,
    years = 2000:2000,
    template = SRTM,                            # run on the SRTM grid
    soil_profile = example_soil_profile(depths),
)

output = solve(problem)
```

::: {.fragment}
- One `solve` call. Threading over pixels happens internally.
- Terrain and Weather are downloaded for the area, and resampled onto the `template` grid; 
  derived variables are calculated (e.g. slope, aspect, horizon angles)
- Same `output_layers` schema as the point case, but this time heatmap-ready Rasters.
:::

---

## Mont Aigoual, Cévennes — July 2000

![](figures/grid_soil_temperature_5cm_drape.png){fig-align="center" width="90%"}

Shaded valleys take longer to warm up, east facing slopes are cooler in the afternoon.

---

## Swap the weather source, change resolution — NCEP daily

```julia
snow_area = Extent(X=(3.33, 3.83), Y=(43.87, 44.37))
# Lets use SRTM as a template, but aggregate to a lower resolution
srtm = Raster(SRTM; extent=snow_area, lazy=true)
template = aggregate(mean, crop(srtm; to=snow_area, touches=true), 6)
map_model = MicroMapModel(micro_model;
    dem_source = SRTM,
    weather_source = NCEP{SurfaceGauss},  # swap out TerraClimate
    surface_albedo_source = 0.15,
    roughness_height_source = 0.004u"m",
)
yearly_problem = MicroRasterProblem(map_model;
    area = snow_area, years = 2010:2010,
    soil_profile = example_soil_profile(depths),
    init = (;
        soil_moisture = fill(0.25, length(depths)),
        snow_depth = 0.0u"cm",
    ),
    template = snow_template,
)
solve(yearly_problem)
```

---

![](figures/grid_snow_depth_yearly.gif){fig-align="center"}

---

## Status & roadmap

::: {.incremental}
- Microclimate.jl is validated against NicheMapR output
- Packages not yet registered, but soon
- Some syntax still in flux
- Cold-air drainage and water runoff models — in progress
- Open source, MIT licensed, contributions welcome!
:::

---

## The package ecosystem

```{dot}
//| fig-width: 10
//| fig-height: 4.5
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent";
  rankdir=LR;
  splines=curved;
  nodesep=0.18;
  ranksep=0.7;
  pad=0.15;
  fontname="Palatino,Charter,Georgia,serif";

  node [
    shape=box,
    style="rounded,filled",
    fillcolor="#fffff8",
    color="#666666",
    fontname="Palatino,Charter,Georgia,serif",
    fontsize=12,
    fontcolor="#111111",
    margin="0.18,0.08",
    penwidth=0.9
  ];

  edge [
    color="#999999",
    penwidth=0.9,
    arrowsize=0.6,
    arrowhead=vee
  ];

  subgraph cluster_base {
    label=<<i>Base</i>>;
    fontsize=13;
    fontcolor="#555555";
    style="dashed,rounded";
    color="#bbbbbb";
    margin=14;
    UF  [label="Unitful.jl"];
    SOL [label="SolarRadiation.jl", color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
    FLU [label="FluidProperties.jl", color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
  }

  subgraph cluster_core {
    label=<<i>Physics core</i>>;
    fontsize=13;
    fontcolor="#555555";
    style="dashed,rounded";
    color="#bbbbbb";
    margin=14;
    MP  [label="ModelParameters.jl"];
    MIC [label=<<b>Microclimate.jl</b>>, color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
    SCI [label="SciML / OrdinaryDiffEq.jl"];
  }

  subgraph cluster_scale {
    label=<<i>Landscape</i>>;
    fontsize=13;
    fontcolor="#555555";
    style="dashed,rounded";
    color="#bbbbbb";
    margin=14;
    RAS [label="Rasters.jl"];
    RDS [label="RasterDataSources.jl"];
    GEO [label="Geomorphometry.jl"];
    MCP [label=<<b>MicroclimateMapper.jl</b>>, color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
  }

  subgraph cluster_eco {
    label=<<i>Ecology models</i>>;
    fontsize=13;
    fontcolor="#555555";
    style="dashed,rounded";
    color="#bbbbbb";
    margin=14;
    BBE [label="BiophysicalBehaviour.jl", color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
    HEX [label="HeatExchange.jl", color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
  }

  subgraph cluster_bio {
    label=<<i>Biogeography models</i>>;
    fontsize=13;
    fontcolor="#555555";
    style="dashed,rounded";
    color="#bbbbbb";
    margin=14;
    SDM [label="SpeciesDistributionModels.jl"];
    ANM [label="AnimalMapper.jl", style="dashed,rounded,filled", color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
    PLM [label="PlantMapper.jl", style="dashed,rounded,filled", color="#a00000", fontcolor="#a00000", penwidth=1.4];
  }

  // Inter-cluster flow (anchored on the core nodes of each layer)
  SOL  -> MIC [ltail=cluster_forcing, lhead=cluster_core];
  FLU  -> MIC [ltail=cluster_forcing, lhead=cluster_core];
  MIC  -> MCP [ltail=cluster_core, lhead=cluster_scale];
  MIC  -> HEX [ltail=cluster_scale, lhead=cluster_eco];
  MCP  -> SDM [ltail=cluster_scale, lhead=cluster_eco];
  BBE  -> ANM [ltail=cluster_scale, lhead=cluster_eco];
  HEX  -> ANM [ltail=cluster_scale, lhead=cluster_eco];
  HEX  -> PLM [ltail=cluster_scale, lhead=cluster_eco];
}
```

::: {.diagram-legend}
[ ]{.swatch .existing} existing &nbsp;&nbsp;
[ ]{.swatch .planned} planned &nbsp;&nbsp;
[ ]{.swatch .external} external
:::

---

## Thanks

::: {.incremental}
- **Mike Kearney** (Melbourne) — NicheMapR, biophysical ecology vision
- Open source contributors: JuliaGeo (spatial data), Makie (plots), SciML (solvers) ecosystems
:::

::: {.fragment}

**Code & slides**: [github.com/BiophysicalEcology](https://github.com/BiophysicalEcology)

:::
